message:
feat: explicit index persistence, compiled INSERT fast path, descending rowid scans, and text function optimizations

A broad set of correctness and performance improvements across four
crates in the FrankenSQLite engine:

## compat_persist.rs — Explicit index round-tripping through sqlite_master

sqlite_master entry tracking now includes the entry type ("table" vs
"index") and tbl_name fields, matching the real SQLite schema format.
This enables:
- Writing CREATE INDEX entries to sqlite_master during persist, not just
  CREATE TABLE entries
- Loading explicit indexes (CREATE INDEX ...) back from sqlite_master
  during load_from_sqlite via a second-pass scan that skips autoindexes
- New init_leaf_index_page() for index B-tree page initialization
- New parse_create_index_sql_to_schema() parser that extracts column
  names, collations, sort directions, uniqueness, and WHERE clauses
  from CREATE INDEX SQL without regex

## connection.rs — Compiled direct INSERT expressions and change tracking optimization

PreparedDirectSimpleInsert now optionally pre-compiles row value
expressions into a PreparedDirectSimpleInsertExpr enum tree (literals,
placeholders, binary/unary ops, casts, collate, is-null). When
available, the compiled path bypasses the full eval_expr_with_subqueries
pipeline, eliminating per-INSERT overhead for parameterized inserts.

Additional optimizations:
- may_observe_change_tracking flag on PreparedStatement: skips the
  expensive sync_change_tracking_context() call for statements that
  never read changes() or last_insert_rowid(), which is the vast
  majority of queries
- memdb_storage_count_shortcuts_safe cell: tracks whether the in-memory
  row mirror is reliable for COUNT(*) shortcuts after VDBE DML mutations
  (write-through DML can leave MemDatabase stale)
- Implicit rowid hint from MemTable: avoids a cursor.last() seek when
  the in-memory table already knows the next rowid
- new_header_btree_cursor for direct inserts: pre-computes page size
  and reserved-per-page at prepare time instead of per-execution

## codegen.rs — Descending rowid range scans and placeholder-safe seek bounds

- Rowid range scans now support ORDER BY rowid DESC natively: uses
  SeekLE/SeekLT + Prev instead of SeekGE/SeekGT + Next, with correct
  bound comparison inversion for the loop termination check
- resolve_order_by_rowid_direction() detects when ORDER BY can be
  satisfied by a rowid scan direction change
- rowid_range_bound_is_seek_safe() now accepts placeholder expressions
  (numbered, colon-named, at-named, dollar-named), not just integer
  literals — enables parameterized range queries to use the fast path
- count_exists_semijoin_merge and extract_safe_probe_source_rowid_range
  for optimized indexed COUNT with IN subqueries

## builtins.rs — Zero-copy text argument extraction

Introduces text_arg() helper returning Cow<str> that borrows directly
from SqliteValue::Text's Arc<str> when possible, avoiding the
to_text() allocation for LOWER, UPPER, SUBSTR, LIKE, and GLOB. Also
adds an ASCII fast path for SUBSTR that skips char-by-char iteration
when the input is pure ASCII.

## engine.rs — MemTable rowid hint and COUNT shortcut gating

- MemTable::next_rowid_hint() exposes the next implicit rowid for the
  direct INSERT fast path
- storage_cursor_memdb_count_shortcuts_safe flag gates COUNT(*) on
  storage cursors to prevent stale counts after DML
- find_scalar_precanonical() used for Function opcode dispatch to avoid
  redundant name canonicalization

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
